Name: Video-linked Thai/Swedish child data corpus
Description: Longitudinal recordings of child-caregiver interaction in everyday contexts. The CLARIN family record describes 60 transcripts from six children in Sweden and Thailand, with videos linked to transcripts on an utterance-by-utterance basis.
CountryOrInstitution: Sweden; Thailand; Lund University Humanities Lab
Notes: Included because Swedish is one of the corpus languages and the resource is already represented in the CLARIN multimodal corpus family.

Name: Svensk teckensprakskorpus / Swedish Sign Language Corpus (STS-korpus)
Description: Web-based corpus tool for searching Swedish Sign Language corpus material. The information page states that video recordings and annotations were produced in the Swedish Sign Language Corpus project and can be downloaded for research and teaching; annotations are intended for use with ELAN.
Infrastructure: Stockholm University; Department of Linguistics; STS-korpus
Notes: The site is in Swedish. Licence details were not stated in the inspected public page; access appears to require registration for some downloads.

Name: RixVox-v2
Description: Swedish parliamentary speech dataset from the National Library of Sweden / KBLab. The Hugging Face record describes audio and text data built by matching and force-aligning parliamentary protocols to media recordings of debates.
Infrastructure: Hugging Face; National Library of Sweden / KBLab
CountryOrInstitution: Sweden; National Library of Sweden / KBLab
Notes: Merged with the earlier RixVox record as the same underlying parliamentary-speech resource family; v2 is listed as the current, larger dataset.

Name: SweDia 2000
Description: Website for listening to recordings of more than 100 Swedish dialects recorded across Sweden and Swedish-speaking parts of Finland. It is included as a Swedish-hosted speech resource with audio linked to dialect/location context.
Infrastructure: University of Gothenburg and Swedish dialect research context
Notes: The landing page clearly exposes audio listening and dialect metadata, but the first-pass inspection did not verify downloadable corpus files or systematic transcription availability.

Name: FLEURS Swedish (sv_se)
Description: Swedish subset of Google's FLEURS multilingual speech dataset. The Hugging Face dataset card lists audio and text modalities, Swedish (sv_se) as one subset, and fields for audio, transcription and raw transcription.
Notes: Included because Swedish is an explicit language subset; not Sweden-hosted.

Name: Mozilla Common Voice Swedish
Description: Crowdsourced speech corpus pairing voice recordings with prompt text and validation metadata. The older Hugging Face record for Common Voice 17.0 states that Common Voice datasets moved to Mozilla Data Collective in October 2025.
Infrastructure: Mozilla Common Voice; Mozilla Data Collective
Notes: Included as a well-known Swedish speech/text resource, but current download and licence details should be rechecked in Mozilla Data Collective because the old Hugging Face dataset record is now empty.

Name: Eye-Tracked Frog Stories
Description: Lund Corpora collection organized into English and Swedish subcollections, with a Swedish branch further organized by images and persons. Included as a Sweden-hosted multimodal/sensor collection linked to eye-tracked narrative material.
Infrastructure: CLARIN; Språkbanken CLARIN; Lund University Humanities Lab; CLARIN-MULTISENS
Notes: The public collection hierarchy confirms English and Swedish branches; detailed licence, size, and file-level annotation should be checked in the archive.

Name: Tactile Reading
Description: Participants read a short text from Pippi Longstocking and feel a tactile image of a face. The LU Humanities Lab Archive description says the material was recorded by an automated finger-tracker; item descriptions mention video generated by finger-tracking, spoken retelling, diagrams and tracked coordinates.
Infrastructure: CLARIN; Språkbanken CLARIN; Lund University Humanities Lab; CLARIN-MULTISENS
Notes: Language is not explicitly encoded in the inspected collection metadata; the Pippi Longstocking text context suggests Swedish relevance but this is not asserted in the Language field.

Name: Strömqvist-Richthoff
Description: Strömqvist-Richthoff collection listed under Lund Corpora. The collection name points to Swedish writing/language acquisition research, but the public listing inspected in this pass did not expose a description.
Notes: Added as a lead from the Lund Corpora inventory; needs file-level verification before strong claims.

Name: Folke
Description: Digital archive service from the Institute for Language and Folklore (Isof) exposing a selection of older archive material about people's lives, work, everyday life, festivities, experiences and memories from the late 19th century onward. The public page says users can search, read, listen and transcribe material; current material includes folk-memory records, questionnaires and recordings.
CountryOrInstitution: Sweden; Institute for Language and Folklore (Isof)
Notes: Added after the user pointed out the missing Isof/Folke resource. It is Swedish-content archival material and clearly multimodal through text/manuscripts, recordings, listening and transcription workflows. Licence/download details were not found on the public overview page.

Name: Isof dialect and folklore recordings / Intervjuer och inspelningar
Description: Isof's collections of spoken-language recordings, mainly interviews documenting dialects and folklore in Sweden. The public page reports roughly 25,000 recorded hours, including around 15,500 gramophone discs, more than 18,000 reel tapes and nearly 9,000 cassettes, with some transcripts available for ordering.
CountryOrInstitution: Sweden; Institute for Language and Folklore (Isof)
Notes: This is a collection-level entry because Isof describes the holdings and access routes at collection scale rather than as one downloadable corpus.

Name: Dialektkartan
Description: Isof map service for listening to hundreds of shorter audio clips from older Swedish dialect recordings from across Sweden, mainly recordings from 1935-1970. Full-length recordings and written records are routed to Folke.
CountryOrInstitution: Sweden; Institute for Language and Folklore (Isof)
Notes: Included as a Swedish dialect audio-plus-map resource; it is a browser/service view rather than a bulk-download corpus.

Name: SpeechDat Swedish (KTH Hugging Face release)
Description: KTH Hugging Face release of the SpeechDat dataset. The dataset repository contains a loader script, metadata JSON, README and a large WAV archive; the README describes loading the dataset with local WAV files in a speechdat/wav directory. This supersedes the earlier ELRA-only catalogue references in this survey for the KTH-accessible SpeechDat release.
Infrastructure: Hugging Face; KTH; Språkbanken Tal; Språkbanken CLARIN
Notes: Replaces the separate ELRA catalogue-only SpeechDat entries after confirming the KTH Hugging Face dataset. The old ELRA records remain useful provenance/distribution references but are no longer the primary survey entry.

Name: Waxholm corpus
Description: Swedish spoken dialogue corpus collected in 1993-1994 at the Department of Speech, Hearing and Music (TMH), KTH. The Hugging Face dataset card describes sampled speech files and label files; labels include orthographic text, phonetic/phonemic labels, word-level labels and alignment information.
Infrastructure: Hugging Face; KTH; Språkbanken Tal; Språkbanken CLARIN
Publication: @article{bertenstam1995spoken, title={Spoken dialogue data collected in the Waxholm project}, journal={STH-QPSR, KTH}, year={1995}}
Notes: Added after user pointed out the Hugging Face release. The repository contains .wav, .smp and .mix files and KTH publications describing the corpus.

Name: Training data for Swedish Lion Libre
Description: Hugging Face collection from Riksarkivet grouping public training data used to create the Swedish Lion Libre HTR model set. The collection contains 11 text-line datasets from Swedish archival sources; Hugging Face dataset-server metadata reports image and text modalities for each listed dataset.
Infrastructure: Hugging Face; Riksarkivet; Swedish National Archives; Språkbanken CLARIN node
Notes: Added as a collection-level entry rather than one record per line dataset. Collection items include goteborgs_poliskammare_fore_1900_lines, svea_hovratt_lines, krigshovrattens_dombocker_lines, bergskollegium_relationer_och_skrivelser_lines, frihetstidens_utskottshandlingar_lines, carl_fredrik_pahlmans_resejournaler_lines, trolldomskommissionen_lines, gota_hovratt_lines, bergmastaren_i_nora_htr_lines, alvsborgs_losen_lines, and jonkopings_radhusratt_och_magistrat_lines. Individual item licences/descriptions may need follow-up.

Name: Swedish Fraktur
Description: Riksarkivet Hugging Face dataset for Swedish 19th-century blackletter. The dataset card says transcriptions were made by Språkbanken and converted into a text-line dataset by the Swedish National Archives; the stated direct use is training text-line OCR models for Swedish 19th-century blackletter.
Infrastructure: Hugging Face; Riksarkivet; Swedish National Archives; Språkbanken
CountryOrInstitution: Sweden; Riksarkivet / Swedish National Archives; Språkbanken
Notes: The Hugging Face card explicitly lists task_categories:image-to-text, language:sv, modality:image, modality:text, and dataset features image and text. It is kept separate from the Swedish Lion Libre collection because the card is well described and has explicit Språkbanken provenance.
